executive_summary: Your supplier risk is driven almost entirely by geopolitical exposure concentrated in three zones: China (six suppliers across EMS, nicotine, batteries, and substrates facing US-China trade and Taiwan risks), South Africa (Sappi under energy crisis pressure), and Eastern Europe/Korea (Stora Enso near Russia, Tae Young near North Korea). The critical-exposure EMS suppliers (Huizhou BYD, Smoore) and high-exposure battery/nicotine suppliers (EVE Energy, ITC) warrant immediate diversification planning—these aren't temporary disruptions but structural policy risks. The Sappi signal is distinct and slower-burning; focus there only after securing alternatives for your China concentration. Macro and peer health remain stable, so this is purely a sourcing resilience issue: task procurement to map substitute suppliers outside these geopolitical flashpoints and cost the premium now rather than face sudden allocation crises.
